threats.title: Forged or replayed tokens, or an unauthenticated endpoint
threats.description: A token is accepted without verifying signature/expiry, or an endpoint simply omits the auth check, letting a caller impersonate a principal.

threats.title: Injection through unvalidated parameters
threats.description: A parameter flows into a SQL query, an OS command, or a template without parameterization/escaping, so the caller alters the intended operation.

threats.title: Privileged API actions are not logged
threats.description: Mutating or administrative calls leave no attributable record, so abuse can't be reconstructed.

threats.title: Excessive data exposure or server-side request forgery
threats.description: An endpoint returns more fields than the client needs (or accepts a URL it then fetches), leaking internal data or reaching internal services.

threats.title: No rate limiting or unbounded query cost
threats.description: An expensive query or a deeply nested GraphQL request runs unthrottled, exhausting the backend.

threats.title: IDOR — object references not scoped to the caller
threats.description: An id in the request selects a record without checking the caller owns it, so changing the id reaches another user's object.
